text.title: langchain-agents-architectures Worked Examples
text.summary: Three worked examples — ReAct, Plan-and-Execute, LATS — with code shape and observed behaviour.

example.code: agent = create_react_agent( ChatAnthropic(model="claude-sonnet-4-7"), tools=[search, calculator], max_iterations=15, ) out = agent.invoke({"messages": [("human", "What is 25 * 4 and who invented calculus?")]})
example.p: Behaviour: 4 iterations — 1 calc, 1 search, 1 reflection, 1 final answer. Total ~1500 tokens. Trace appears in LangSmith with session_id.

example.title: StateGraph with planner / executor / synthesizer
example.p: Planner produces 4-step plan as JSON; executor runs each step with tools; synthesizer combines results. Re-plan gate fires after step 2 when search returned empty — planner adjusts steps 3-4 to use alternative queries. Total: 6 LLM calls, ~5K tokens, p99 ~12s.

example.title: Tree search for novel problem
example.p: 2 branches × 3 depth. Score with Haiku, expand with Sonnet. 8 LLM calls total; best score 0.87 found at depth 2; backtracked once when initial branch scored below 0.5.

example.title: Unbounded ReAct on undocumented tool
example.p: Agent calls a tool whose docstring lacks "Do NOT use for" hints; tool is misapplied; agent enters retry loop. Without `max_iterations`, the loop runs 50+ iterations before user kills it. Fix: tighten docstring; set max_iterations=15.
